title: Fantasy Map Maker - Improved Edition - Cozy Map Making Roll and Write
body:
Daniel (@dungeondive) explores Fantasy Map Maker: Improved Edition, an award-winning roll-and-write from designer Harry Metcalf where you create fantasy maps by rolling dice and drawing terrain features across a hex-style grid.

The gameplay loop is satisfying: roll 2d6, assign one die to select a wheel wedge and the other to determine the size/length of your feature, then draw coastlines, mountains, forests, rivers, and settlements. Five randomised quest cards set your scoring criteria each game — you might be rewarded for mountains with peaks on all four corners, deep forests, sprawling coastlines, or settlements near water.

What makes it interesting:

  • Landmarks (castles, keeps, temples, mines) give you special powers to manipulate dice or ignore bad rolls
  • Event cards add fantasy elements like meteor impacts, trolls, or necromancers
  • The maps you create actually make geographic sense and could be used for solo RPG adventures
  • A continent expansion lets you combine multiple maps for epic-scale worldbuilding

The Improved Edition is available now on itch.io, with a deluxe print version hitting Kickstarter in February 2026. Daniel also teases the upcoming Dungeon Map Maker — same system, but you build a dungeon then explore it with a character.

Have you tried any map-making games? Do you use generated maps in your solo RPG campaigns?
